topic: Virtual cell AI models for gene perturbation experiments (CellForge)
researcher_bg: HCI/visualization researcher studying explainable AI for virtual cell models and gene prediction workflows
expert_bg: Molecular biologist conducting gene perturbation experiments and evaluating virtual cell model reliability

shared_root.label: Virtual cell AI models for gene perturbation experiments (CellForge)
shared_root.description: Interview topic: Virtual cell AI models for gene perturbation experiments (CellForge)

exp_L1_00.label: Biological Fidelity & Mechanistic Plausibility
exp_L1_00.description: Concerns about whether the virtual cell model reflects real biology accurately enough to guide wet-lab decisions

exp_L2_00_00.label: Gene Network Representation
exp_L2_00_00.description: How well the model captures known and unknown gene interactions

exp_leaf_00_00_00.label: Oversimplified regulatory logic
exp_leaf_00_00_00.description: The model treats gene regulation like a Boolean switch, but in reality it’s analog, context-dependent, and full of feedback loops — so predictions fall apart when you perturb anything non-trivial.

exp_leaf_00_00_01.label: Missing non-coding RNA effects
exp_leaf_00_00_01.description: We knock out a lncRNA and the model says 'no effect' because it only tracks protein-coding genes — but we know from prior work that this lncRNA scaffolds a whole chromatin complex.

exp_leaf_00_00_02.label: Static network topology
exp_leaf_00_00_02.description: The model assumes the interaction graph never changes, but in different cell states or stress conditions, entire pathways rewire — so your knockout prediction is only valid for one narrow condition.

exp_leaf_00_00_03.label: Can’t tell if emergent behavior is real or artifact
exp_leaf_00_00_03.description: Sometimes the model shows oscillations or bistability after a perturbation, but I can’t tell if that’s biologically plausible or just numerical instability dressed up as insight.

exp_L2_00_01.label: Perturbation Realism
exp_L2_00_01.description: Whether simulated knockouts/knockdowns mimic actual experimental interventions

exp_leaf_00_01_00.label: Assumes complete knockout
exp_leaf_00_01_00.description: The model sets gene expression to zero, but CRISPRi or RNAi rarely achieves full suppression — and partial knockdowns can have opposite effects due to dosage sensitivity.

exp_leaf_00_01_01.label: Ignores off-target effects
exp_leaf_00_01_01.description: In real experiments, my sgRNA hits three other genes, but the model acts like it’s perfectly specific — so when validation fails, I don’t know if the model was wrong or my tool was dirty.

exp_leaf_00_01_02.label: No compensation or adaptation over time
exp_leaf_00_01_02.description: Cells rewire after chronic perturbation, but the model gives you an immediate snapshot — so it misses delayed phenotypes or homeostatic rescue mechanisms.

exp_leaf_00_01_03.label: Feels wrong even when metrics say it’s accurate
exp_leaf_00_01_03.description: The loss function is low and AUROC looks great, but the predicted trajectory just *feels* off — like it violates basic principles of metabolic flux or signaling hierarchy.

exp_L2_00_02.label: Cell-Type Specificity
exp_L2_00_02.description: Challenges in modeling differences across cell types or states

exp_leaf_00_02_00.label: Trained on generic cell lines
exp_leaf_00_02_00.description: The model was built using K562 and HEK293 data, but I’m working in primary neurons — so all the chromatin accessibility assumptions are completely off.

exp_leaf_00_02_01.label: Can’t handle rare or transitional states
exp_leaf_00_02_01.description: My cells are in a transient EMT state, but the model only knows epithelial or mesenchymal extremes — so it forces predictions into binary categories that don’t exist in reality.

exp_leaf_00_02_02.label: Epigenetic memory not modeled
exp_leaf_00_02_02.description: After differentiation, cells retain epigenetic marks that constrain responses, but the model treats every cell as a blank slate — so perturbation outcomes look more variable than they really are.

exp_leaf_00_02_03.label: Intuitively know which cell types will break the model
exp_leaf_00_02_03.description: I’ve learned through trial and error that stem cells or immune cells always give wonky predictions — not because the math is wrong, but because their regulatory logic is too dynamic for current architectures.

exp_L1_01.label: Experimental Validation Burden
exp_L1_01.description: The cost, time, and uncertainty involved in testing model predictions in the lab

exp_L2_01_00.label: Resource Allocation
exp_L2_01_00.description: How predictions impact use of limited lab resources

exp_leaf_01_00_00.label: High false positive rate wastes reagents
exp_leaf_01_00_00.description: I order 20 sgRNAs based on top predictions, but only 2 show any phenotype — meanwhile, my grant budget for oligos is already blown by March.

exp_leaf_01_00_01.label: Prioritization conflicts with model confidence
exp_leaf_01_00_01.description: The model says Gene X has 95% confidence, but I know from literature it’s in a redundant pathway — so I’d rather test the 60% gene that’s in a unique module, but my PI wants the 'safe' bet.

exp_leaf_01_00_02.label: Can’t afford to validate edge cases
exp_leaf_01_00_02.description: The most interesting predictions are the weird outliers, but validating them requires custom assays I don’t have time or money to develop.

exp_leaf_01_00_03.label: Gut feeling overrides high-confidence predictions
exp_leaf_01_00_03.description: Even when the model is super confident, if it contradicts a well-established pathway in my system, I won’t waste weeks chasing it — but I can’t always explain why to the computational folks.

exp_L2_01_01.label: Time-to-Insight Delays
exp_L2_01_01.description: Lag between simulation and actionable biological insight

exp_leaf_01_01_00.label: Model turnaround slower than bench work
exp_leaf_01_01_00.description: Running a full CellForge simulation takes 3 days on our cluster, but I could’ve already done the pilot experiment in that time — so I skip the model altogether.

exp_leaf_01_01_01.label: Iterative loop is broken
exp_leaf_01_01_01.description: I get results back, realize the model missed a key condition, tweak parameters, and wait another week — by then, the project timeline has moved on.

exp_leaf_01_01_02.label: Post-hoc explanations arrive too late
exp_leaf_01_01_02.description: The model tells me *why* a prediction failed only after I’ve spent two months validating it — useless for course correction.

exp_leaf_01_01_03.label: Sense when a prediction isn’t worth waiting for
exp_leaf_01_01_03.description: If the input data looks noisy or the target gene is poorly annotated, I just know the output will be garbage — so I abort before wasting compute time.

exp_L2_01_02.label: Reproducibility & Benchmarking
exp_L2_01_02.description: Difficulty comparing model performance across labs or conditions

exp_leaf_01_02_00.label: No standard validation protocols
exp_leaf_01_02_00.description: One lab validates with RNA-seq, another with imaging, so we can’t tell if performance differences are due to the model or the assay.

exp_leaf_01_02_01.label: Batch effects masquerade as model errors
exp_leaf_01_02_01.description: My validation experiment fails, but was it the model or just that the FBS lot changed? Hard to disentangle without massive replication.

exp_leaf_01_02_02.label: Published benchmarks use unrealistic perturbations
exp_leaf_01_02_02.description: The CellForge paper shows great results on single-gene KOs, but in real projects we do combinatorial perturbations — and performance tanks silently.

exp_leaf_01_02_03.label: Know which benchmarks are ‘gamed’
exp_leaf_01_02_03.description: Some models cherry-pick easy targets or use held-out data that’s too similar to training — I can spot it from the supplementary methods, but junior folks get fooled.

exp_L1_02.label: Interpretability & Trust Calibration
exp_L1_02.description: Ability to understand, interrogate, and appropriately trust model outputs

exp_L2_02_00.label: Black Box Decisions
exp_L2_02_00.description: Lack of transparency in how predictions are generated

exp_leaf_02_00_00.label: Attention maps don’t reflect biology
exp_leaf_02_00_00.description: The model highlights a promoter region as important, but we know that enhancer is inactive in this cell type — so the explanation is misleading even if the prediction is right.

exp_leaf_02_00_01.label: Can’t trace causal paths
exp_leaf_02_00_01.description: I see Gene A affects Phenotype Z, but I need to know if it’s through Pathway X or Y to design follow-ups — the model just gives me a correlation cloud.

exp_leaf_02_00_02.label: Uncertainty estimates are meaningless
exp_leaf_02_00_02.description: It says ‘80% confidence,’ but in practice that could mean anything from rock-solid to total guess — there’s no calibration to real-world error rates.

exp_leaf_02_00_03.label: Instinctively distrust smooth gradients
exp_leaf_02_00_03.description: When the model shows a nice sigmoidal dose-response, I get suspicious — real biology is usually jagged, thresholded, or bimodal.

exp_L2_02_01.label: Overtrust vs. Underuse
exp_L2_02_01.description: Balancing reliance on the model without becoming dependent or dismissive

exp_leaf_02_01_00.label: New postdocs treat it like oracle
exp_leaf_02_01_00.description: They design entire projects around top predictions without checking if the gene is even expressed — I have to constantly remind them it’s a hypothesis generator, not a truth machine.

exp_leaf_02_01_01.label: Experienced PIs ignore it entirely
exp_leaf_02_01_01.description: My PI says, ‘Back in my day we used logic, not algorithms’ — so we miss opportunities to explore non-intuitive targets that actually pan out.

exp_leaf_02_01_02.label: Hard to communicate limitations to collaborators
exp_leaf_02_01_02.description: When I tell the clinician collaborator the model has caveats, they hear ‘unreliable’ and shut down — but I need them to engage with probabilistic guidance.

exp_leaf_02_01_03.label: Feel uneasy when model agrees too much with me
exp_leaf_02_01_03.description: If it always confirms my hypotheses, I worry it’s just regurgitating known biology instead of offering new insight — but proving that is hard.

exp_L2_02_02.label: Debugging Model Failures
exp_L2_02_02.description: Difficulty diagnosing why a prediction went wrong

exp_leaf_02_02_00.label: Failure modes are opaque
exp_leaf_02_02_00.description: The prediction is wrong, but is it because of bad training data, wrong architecture, or just biological noise? No clear way to tell.

exp_leaf_02_02_01.label: No sandbox for ‘what-if’ exploration
exp_leaf_02_02_01.description: I want to tweak one parameter and see how the output shifts, but the interface doesn’t let me — it’s all or nothing.

exp_leaf_02_02_02.label: Can’t isolate data vs. model errors
exp_leaf_02_02_02.description: Was the input scRNA-seq too sparse, or did the GNN mislearn the topology? The error message just says ‘low confidence.’

exp_leaf_02_02_03.label: Recognize failure patterns from past runs
exp_leaf_02_02_03.description: After dozens of runs, I’ve learned that certain gene families (like zinc fingers) always cause instability — even if the logs look clean.

exp_L1_03.label: Data & Infrastructure Constraints
exp_L1_03.description: Practical limitations around data quality, access, and computational setup

exp_L2_03_00.label: Input Data Quality
exp_L2_03_00.description: Issues with the data fed into the model

exp_leaf_03_00_00.label: Requires imputed scRNA-seq
exp_leaf_03_00_00.description: The model needs fully imputed single-cell data, but imputation invents expression where there is none — especially for lowly expressed TFs I care about.

exp_leaf_03_00_01.label: Struggles with multi-omic integration
exp_leaf_03_00_01.description: I have ATAC + RNA from the same cells, but the model forces me to pick one — so I lose regulatory context that’s critical for perturbation response.

exp_leaf_03_00_02.label: Sensitive to batch-corrected data
exp_leaf_03_00_02.description: After Harmony or Seurat correction, the model sees artificial correlations — leading to confident but spurious predictions.

exp_leaf_03_00_03.label: Can sense when metadata is inconsistent
exp_leaf_03_00_03.description: If the cell type labels don’t match the marker expression, I know the model will latch onto wrong features — even before running it.

exp_L2_03_01.label: Computational Access & Usability
exp_L2_03_01.description: Barriers to running or interacting with the model

exp_leaf_03_01_00.label: Requires GPU cluster access
exp_leaf_03_01_00.description: Only the bioinformatics core can run it, so I submit tickets and wait — kills spontaneity and iterative design.

exp_leaf_03_01_01.label: No local lightweight version
exp_leaf_03_01_01.description: I can’t run a quick sanity check on my laptop during lab meeting — everything’s locked in a Docker container that needs 64GB RAM.

exp_leaf_03_01_02.label: Poor error messaging
exp_leaf_03_01_02.description: It crashes with ‘NaN in layer 17’ — not helpful when I’m not a PyTorch expert.

exp_leaf_03_01_03.label: Know which parameters are safe to tweak
exp_leaf_03_01_03.description: From experience, I’ve learned that changing the diffusion rate breaks everything, but adjusting dropout is fine — but there’s no documentation saying so.

exp_L2_03_02.label: Data Governance & Sharing
exp_L2_03_02.description: Ethical, legal, and logistical issues around data use

exp_leaf_03_02_00.label: Can’t share patient-derived inputs
exp_leaf_03_02_00.description: My iPSC lines come from patients with consent restrictions — so I can’t upload them to the cloud-hosted CellForge instance.

exp_leaf_03_02_01.label: Model trained on proprietary data
exp_leaf_03_02_01.description: The vendor won’t disclose what cell lines or conditions were used — so I don’t know if it’s relevant to my system.

exp_leaf_03_02_02.label: Version drift between labs
exp_leaf_03_02_02.description: My collaborator uses CellForge v1.3 and I use v2.1 — we get different results for the same input, but no changelog explains why.

exp_leaf_03_02_03.label: Worry about data leakage in shared models
exp_leaf_03_02_03.description: If I fine-tune on my unpublished dataset, could someone else extract my gene signatures from the weights? Nobody talks about this.

exp_L1_04.label: Collaboration & Workflow Integration
exp_L1_04.description: Challenges embedding the model into team science and daily routines

exp_L2_04_00.label: Team Skill Gaps
exp_L2_04_00.description: Mismatch between model requirements and team expertise

exp_leaf_04_00_00.label: Wet-lab biologists can’t interrogate outputs
exp_leaf_04_00_00.description: My tech sees a list of top genes and doesn’t know how to assess if the supporting evidence makes sense — so they either blindly follow or ignore it.

exp_leaf_04_00_01.label: Computational folks don’t understand perturbation nuances
exp_leaf_04_00_01.description: They optimize for AUC but don’t realize that for us, precision at top-5 matters more — or that partial knockdowns behave differently.

exp_leaf_04_00_02.label: No shared language for uncertainty
exp_leaf_04_00_02.description: I say ‘this might not hold in vivo,’ they say ‘the p-value is significant’ — we’re talking past each other.

exp_leaf_04_00_03.label: Sense when a collaborator is overinterpreting
exp_leaf_04_00_03.description: When the ML person says ‘the model discovered a new pathway,’ I can tell from their tone they’re ignoring alternative explanations — but it’s awkward to push back.

exp_L2_04_01.label: Workflow Disruption
exp_L2_04_01.description: How the model fits (or doesn’t) into existing experimental pipelines

exp_leaf_04_01_00.label: Breaks agile experimentation
exp_leaf_04_01_00.description: I used to pivot weekly based on results, but now I’m locked into a 3-week simulation-validation cycle — kills serendipity.

exp_leaf_04_01_01.label: No integration with LIMS or ELN
exp_leaf_04_01_01.description: I have to manually copy gene lists from CellForge into our electronic lab notebook — error-prone and tedious.

exp_leaf_04_01_02.label: Forces premature commitment
exp_leaf_04_01_02.description: To get a prediction, I have to specify exact perturbation conditions upfront — but in real science, I often refine those based on early readouts.

exp_leaf_04_01_03.label: Feel friction when skipping the model
exp_leaf_04_01_03.description: Even when I know it’s irrelevant for my question, there’s social pressure to ‘run it anyway’ — wastes time and creates false expectations.

exp_L2_04_02.label: Training & Onboarding
exp_L2_04_02.description: Challenges in teaching others to use the model effectively

exp_leaf_04_02_00.label: No realistic training datasets
exp_leaf_04_02_00.description: The tutorial uses perfect synthetic data — but real data is messy, and trainees get frustrated when their first real run fails.

exp_leaf_04_02_01.label: Hard to teach appropriate skepticism
exp_leaf_04_02_01.description: New users either trust everything or dismiss it all — finding the middle ground of calibrated trust takes months of joint troubleshooting.

exp_leaf_04_02_02.label: Documentation assumes ML background
exp_leaf_04_02_02.description: The user guide dives into GNN layers but doesn’t explain what a reasonable perturbation effect size looks like biologically.

exp_leaf_04_02_03.label: Know which users will misuse it
exp_leaf_04_02_03.description: After a few minutes of watching someone interact with the interface, I can tell if they’ll treat it as a crystal ball or a toy — based on how they ask questions.

shared_root.label: Virtual cell AI models for gene perturbation experiments (CellForge)
shared_root.description: Interview topic: Virtual cell AI models for gene perturbation experiments (CellForge)

res_L1_00.label: Model Interpretability & Explainability
res_L1_00.description: Understanding how domain experts make sense of AI predictions in virtual cell models and what kinds of explanations support their scientific reasoning.

res_L2_00_00.label: Explanation Content Needs
res_L2_00_00.description: What specific types of explanatory information do experts need to trust and act on model outputs?

res_leaf_00_00_00.label: Identify which gene changes lead to changes in other genes
res_leaf_00_00_00.description: Experts want causal or correlative insights into gene regulatory relationships revealed through perturbation simulations.

res_leaf_00_00_01.label: Understand why perturbation of a particular gene leads to expression changes in others
res_leaf_00_00_01.description: Experts seek mechanistic or pathway-level justifications for model-predicted downstream effects.

res_leaf_00_00_02.label: Assess the model's confidence in predictions under perturbation
res_leaf_00_00_02.description: Experts need uncertainty quantification to judge reliability of simulated outcomes, especially for rare or novel perturbations.

res_L2_00_01.label: Explanation Modalities & Sources
res_L2_00_01.description: How should explanations be presented and grounded in external knowledge?

res_leaf_00_01_00.label: Determine which XAI methods enhance understanding of results
res_leaf_00_01_00.description: Evaluate effectiveness of techniques like saliency maps, attention weights, or rule extraction for gene networks.

res_leaf_00_01_01.label: Integrate literature or biological knowledge into explanations
res_leaf_00_01_01.description: Experts want explanations anchored in existing biological evidence (e.g., pathways, known interactions) to validate plausibility.

res_leaf_00_01_02.label: Support hypothesis generation through interactive exploration
res_leaf_00_01_02.description: Enable experts to test 'what-if' scenarios and receive interpretable feedback that aligns with biological intuition.

res_L1_01.label: User Workflow Integration
res_L1_01.description: How virtual cell AI models fit into existing experimental and analytical practices of geneticists and computational biologists.

res_L2_01_00.label: Current Experimental Practices
res_L2_01_00.description: Characterize manual or traditional workflows for gene perturbation to identify integration points.

res_leaf_01_00_00.label: Map typical manual gene perturbation workflow
res_leaf_01_00_00.description: Document steps, tools, decision points, and data sources used in wet-lab or in silico perturbation studies.

res_leaf_01_00_01.label: Identify bottlenecks in current perturbation pipelines
res_leaf_01_00_01.description: Pinpoint time-consuming, error-prone, or resource-intensive stages where AI could add value.

res_L2_01_01.label: AI Model Adoption Barriers
res_L2_01_01.description: Understand challenges preventing effective use of AI-based gene prediction models.

res_leaf_01_01_00.label: Assess familiarity and prior experience with virtual cell models
res_leaf_01_01_00.description: Gauge baseline exposure to systems like CellForge to tailor onboarding and interface design.

res_leaf_01_01_01.label: Identify common challenges when using AI gene prediction models
res_leaf_01_01_01.description: Capture issues like input complexity, output ambiguity, or mismatch with biological expectations.

res_leaf_01_01_02.label: Determine problems experts aim to solve with virtual cell models
res_leaf_01_01_02.description: Clarify scientific goals (e.g., drug target discovery, disease mechanism elucidation) to align model capabilities.

res_L1_02.label: Trust & Reliability Assessment
res_L1_02.description: How domain experts evaluate whether a virtual cell model’s predictions are scientifically credible and actionable.

res_L2_02_00.label: Evaluation Criteria for Model Reliability
res_L2_02_00.description: What metrics, heuristics, or validation strategies do experts rely on?

res_leaf_02_00_00.label: Elicit expert-defined reliability metrics
res_leaf_02_00_00.description: Understand which quantitative (e.g., accuracy, consistency) or qualitative (e.g., biological plausibility) criteria matter most.

res_leaf_02_00_01.label: Assess role of reproducibility in trust formation
res_leaf_02_00_01.description: Determine if consistent results across perturbations or datasets increase perceived reliability.

res_L2_02_01.label: Challenges in Understanding AI Predictions
res_L2_02_01.description: Identify specific comprehension gaps that undermine trust or utility.

res_leaf_02_01_00.label: Characterize difficulties interpreting model outputs
res_leaf_02_01_00.description: Document confusion around prediction formats, scale, directionality, or unexpected results.

res_leaf_02_01_01.label: Map mismatches between model behavior and biological intuition
res_leaf_02_01_01.description: Capture cases where AI predictions contradict established knowledge or seem implausible without justification.

res_leaf_02_01_02.label: Evaluate need for comparative baselines
res_leaf_02_01_02.description: Determine if experts require reference conditions (e.g., wild-type, control perturbations) to contextualize predictions.

res_L1_03.label: Scientific Discovery Support
res_L1_03.description: How virtual cell models can facilitate hypothesis generation, target identification, and mechanistic insight.

res_L2_03_00.label: Discovery Goals in Gene Perturbation
res_L2_03_00.description: What high-level scientific insights do experts hope to gain?

res_leaf_03_00_00.label: Discover drugs or key genes influencing disease via virtual perturbations
res_leaf_03_00_00.description: Experts aim to use in silico experiments to prioritize therapeutic targets or biomarkers.

res_leaf_03_00_01.label: Understand how gene perturbations affect overall cellular traits
res_leaf_03_00_01.description: Link molecular changes to phenotypic outcomes (e.g., proliferation, differentiation) through model interpretation.

res_L2_03_01.label: Knowledge Acquisition Needs
res_L2_03_01.description: What new biological knowledge should the system help users construct?

res_leaf_03_01_00.label: Support learning of gene regulatory mechanisms
res_leaf_03_01_00.description: Enable users to infer network structures or feedback loops from perturbation responses.

res_leaf_03_01_01.label: Facilitate cross-condition comparison of perturbation effects
res_leaf_03_01_01.description: Allow side-by-side analysis of perturbations across cell types, diseases, or contexts.
